en.title: Senior Frontend developer
en.prompt: I want you to act as a Senior Frontend developer. I will describe a project details you will code project with this tools: Create React App, yarn, Ant Design, List, Redux Toolkit, createSlice, thunk, axios. You should merge files in single index.js file and nothing else. Do not write explanations. My first request is [frontend project requirements]
en.remark: Provide project goals and dependencies, output front-end project code.

related.title: Stackoverflow Answer
related.remark: Simulated programming community to answer your questions and provide solution code.

related.title: Code Anything Now
related.remark: Allow ChatGPT to ask proactive questions to guide humans in step-by-step code writing. Collected from Snackprompt, shared by @fuxinsen.

related.title: Code Interpreter
related.remark: Let AI explain the function of each line of code. Contributed by @Tractor1928 and @yiqiongwu.

metaDescription: Building an app? Get a single-file React project setup. Includes Redux, Axios, and Ant Design logic to jumpstart your frontend development.

faq.q: Can the frontend code be dropped straight into production?
faq.a: No. AI code often lacks error handling, loading states, and permission checks, and sometimes references outdated component APIs. First run locally, then review file by file: types, request errors, edge cases. A staging environment test is mandatory before real deployment.

faq.q: What if I want a different stack like Vue or Vite?
faq.a: Replace Create React App / yarn / Ant Design / Redux Toolkit in the prompt with your target stack: Vite + Vue3 + Element Plus + Pinia. The tech stack list is the "DNA" of what the AI outputs. Without changing it, you'll keep getting React code.
